[Leontien de Waal](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/leontien-de-waal/index.html). Architect Paul de Ruiter won the ABN AMRO Sustainable 50 award and consultant Jip van Grinsven the Young Professional award. Their mission? Changing the image of sustainability: no nonsense, because smart building is simply good business. Paul designs buildings that use energy and materials more intelligently.

metadata.source_title: From goat's wool socks to pure profit

text: Architect Paul de Ruiter won the ABN AMRO Sustainable 50 award and consultant Jip van Grinsven the Young Professional award. Their mission? Changing the image of sustainability: no nonsense, because smart building is simply good business. Paul designs buildings that use energy and materials more intelligently. Jip provides insight into the financial side of circularity.

metadata.source_title: From goat's wool socks to pure profit

text: Their mission? Changing the image of sustainability: no nonsense, because smart building is simply good business. Paul designs buildings that use energy and materials more intelligently. Jip provides insight into the financial side of circularity. Based on their own expertise, they show that we should not see sustainability as an expensive obstacle, but as a valuable matter of course.

metadata.source_title: From goat's wool socks to pure profit

text: Paul designs buildings that use energy and materials more intelligently. Jip provides insight into the financial side of circularity. Based on their own expertise, they show that we should not see sustainability as an expensive obstacle, but as a valuable matter of course. Ahead of its time. In 1992, Paul started doing his PhD on energy-efficient buildings. “After two years, my supervisor said: maybe you should start making them.” So he founded his architectural firm in 1994, fully focused on sustainability.

text: Ahead of its time. metadata.source_title: From goat's wool socks to pure profit

text: In 1992, Paul started doing his PhD on energy-efficient buildings. “After two years, my supervisor said: maybe you should start making them.” So he founded his architectural firm in 1994, fully focused on sustainability. 'I had read the Global 2000 Report, in which US President Carter predicted what climate change would cause. At that time, every architect was still concerned with the beauty of buildings. Important, but I didn't think that was enough: responsible construction makes a building even more beautiful.'. At that time, according to Paul, sustainability still had a goat-wool image.

text:
Paul de Ruiter. Founder and director of Paul de Ruiter Architects
From skeptic to pioneer. Jip did not immediately feel strongly about the subject. “During my training, sustainability always came last, it was never really part of it,” she says. It wasn't until she graduated that her perspective changed. 'I discovered that it actually offers economic opportunities.' At consultancy firm Alba Concepts she specialized in the financial side of circular construction.

metadata.source_title: From goat's wool socks to pure profit

text:
Founder and director of Paul de Ruiter Architects
From skeptic to pioneer. Jip did not immediately feel strongly about the subject. “During my training, sustainability always came last, it was never really part of it,” she says. It wasn't until she graduated that her perspective changed. 'I discovered that it actually offers economic opportunities.' At consultancy firm Alba Concepts she specialized in the financial side of circular construction. For example, she created a brochure in which she compares the circularity of various housing construction concepts.

metadata.source_title: From goat's wool socks to pure profit

text: Their work shows that sustainability is a wise choice. A good example is the building that Paul designed for the Erasmus University Rotterdam: 8,500 square meters for three thousand students. 'Ventilation is an energy guzzler in such a building. We have developed a system that works with solar and wind power. The result: 80 to 85 percent energy savings, fewer installations, more fresh air.'.

text: The result: 80 to 85 percent energy savings, fewer installations, more fresh air.'. Jip's innovation lies in his accounting. 'We usually completely depreciate buildings over forty years. But with circular construction you are left with products that still have value. So you do not depreciate to zero euros, but to a certain residual value.

metadata.source_title: From goat's wool socks to pure profit

text: Jip's innovation lies in his accounting. 'We usually completely depreciate buildings over forty years. But with circular construction you are left with products that still have value. So you do not depreciate to zero euros, but to a certain residual value. This will reduce your annual costs.'. > “Installations have a significant CO2 footprint and must be replaced every fifteen years.

metadata.source_title: From goat's wool socks to pure profit

text:
Founder and director of Paul de Ruiter Architects
Nobody wants a stupid building. Both are annoyed by the misunderstanding that sustainability is slow and expensive. “For example, you can save a significant amount of concrete by building smarter, which saves time and money,” says Paul. 'In addition, installations have a significant CO2 footprint and must be replaced every fifteen years. If you design smartly, fewer are needed. And that saves money, maintenance and replacement.'.

metadata.source_title: From goat's wool socks to pure profit

text: Both are annoyed by the misunderstanding that sustainability is slow and expensive. “For example, you can save a significant amount of concrete by building smarter, which saves time and money,” says Paul. Failure costs are also a problem.

metadata.source_title: From goat's wool socks to pure profit

text: Jip focuses on appraisers. 'Valuation only looks back, but we should look forward. Not only accountants have to take circularity into account, appraisers too. Circularity requires a future-oriented view. That is why we are setting up a new sustainable valuation and real estate agency at Alba Concepts in January 2026: Waerdig.

metadata.source_title: From goat's wool socks to pure profit

text:
'Valuation only looks back, but we should look forward. Not only accountants have to take circularity into account, appraisers too. Circularity requires a future-oriented view. That is why we are setting up a new sustainable valuation and real estate agency at Alba Concepts in January 2026: Waerdig. Purely to show that it is possible.' According to both, sustainability should become self-evident. “Just like “the economy,”' says Paul. “You cannot be for or against the economy, it is just there.”
About the ABN AMRO Sustainable 50.

About the ABN AMRO Sustainable 50. The ABN AMRO Sustainable 50 is the list of professionals who demonstrably contribute to a more sustainable built environment. They are the drivers, connectors, accelerators and innovators in the sector. Every year we put these people on stage to honor their work, commitment and vision and to inspire others to get moving. There are awards in three categories: maker, inventor and doer.

text: The ABN AMRO Sustainable 50 is the list of professionals who demonstrably contribute to a more sustainable built environment. They are the drivers, connectors, accelerators and innovators in the sector. Every year we put these people on stage to honor their work, commitment and vision and to inspire others to get moving. There are awards in three categories: maker, inventor and doer. The winners were announced on November 5, 2025 during the Climate Summit for the Built Environment.

text:
The winners were announced on November 5, 2025 during the Climate Summit for the Built Environment. Read more in the construction sector. The Dutch construction sector has been one of the best performing sectors in recent years, despite several challenges facing the sector. Examples are the nitrogen dossier, the significant price increases for energy and building materials and also the personnel shortages. Driven by trends, innovations and legislation and regulations, the relevance of themes such as sustainability and environmental impact is growing and the sector is looking for a new balance.
